question: What did we conclude was causing 403 errors when the frontend loaded static assets from S3?
turns: Users are seeing broken images and missing CSS. Browser devtools show HTTP 403 Forbidden for every request to our assets.example-cdn.com S3 bucket. Started after infra applied a new bucket policy yesterday afternoon.
The bucket is in us-east-1. Objects exist — I can download them via the AWS Console with my IAM credentials. The 403s only affect unauthenticated public HTTP requests from browsers.

Inspected the new bucket policy JSON. The previous policy had a Statement granting Effect: Allow, Principal: *, Action: s3:GetObject for the entire bucket. The updated policy removed that statement entirely — it now only allows access from a specific IAM role, blocking all public reads.

Re-added the s3:GetObject allow for Principal: * scoped to the /assets/* prefix. 403s cleared immediately. Root cause: bucket policy update removed the public s3:GetObject permission for anonymous access.
